problems.title: Origin of Neutrino Masses
problems.tagline: Why do neutrinos have mass, and are they Dirac or Majorana?
problems.summary: Determine the mechanism generating neutrino masses and whether neutrinos are their own antiparticles.
problems.description: Neutrino flavour oscillations, confirmed by Super-Kamiokande and SNO, established that neutrinos have nonzero mass — a clear departure from the Standard Model. The central open question is whether neutrinos are Dirac fermions (distinct from antineutrinos) or Majorana fermions (their own antiparticles), and by what mechanism their extraordinarily small masses arise. The seesaw mechanism, radiative models, and extra-dimensional schemes are all viable. Next-generation neutrinoless double-beta-decay experiments (LEGEND-1000, nEXO, KamLAND-Zen) aim to settle the Dirac/Majorana question within a decade, while cosmological mass bounds from CMB-S4 and DESI further constrain the absolute scale.
problems.abstract: This project targets the origin of neutrino masses, combining theoretical model-building with phenomenological analysis of upcoming experimental data. We will (1) systematically compare seesaw Type I/II/III, radiative, and Dirac-mass scenarios against global oscillation, cosmological, and 0νββ constraints, (2) develop effective field theory templates that map 0νββ half-life sensitivities onto underlying lepton-number-violating operators, and (3) build Bayesian inference pipelines that fold LEGEND, nEXO and KamLAND-Zen projected sensitivities into posterior distributions over mass hierarchy and mechanism. Successful identification of the mass mechanism would provide the first confirmed physics beyond the Standard Model and illuminate the origin of leptogenesis.

problems.title: Strong CP Problem and Axion Detection
problems.tagline: Why is QCD so CP-symmetric, and can the axion be detected?
problems.summary: Confirm or rule out the Peccei-Quinn axion solution to the strong CP problem.
problems.description: The QCD θ-term should generate an observable neutron electric dipole moment, yet experiments bound |θ| < 10⁻¹⁰. The Peccei-Quinn mechanism elegantly drives θ to zero via a light pseudoscalar — the axion — which is also a leading dark matter candidate. Experiments including ADMX, ABRACADABRA, HAYSTAC, and CAPP are actively scanning the μeV–meV mass window with haloscope cavities and broadband magnetometers. Upgrades in cryogenic amplifiers and quantum squeezing are rapidly narrowing the parameter space.
problems.abstract: We pursue the axion as a simultaneous solution to the strong CP problem and the dark matter puzzle. The program combines (1) theoretical refinement of the axion mass/coupling range under high-scale inflation and post-inflationary PQ-breaking scenarios, (2) analysis pipeline development for ADMX Gen-2 and ABRACADABRA data using matched-filter and stochastic-signal estimators, and (3) study of axion-photon mixing in astrophysical magnetic fields to extract complementary bounds from NGC 1275 and similar sources. Detection would resolve a 50-year-old naturalness problem and identify dark matter simultaneously.

problems.title: Equation of State of Neutron Star Cores
problems.tagline: What is the matter state at 5-10 times nuclear saturation density?
problems.summary: Determine the dense-matter equation of state inside neutron stars from multi-messenger data.
problems.description: Neutron star cores reach densities several times nuclear saturation, where quark deconfinement, hyperons, and pion/kaon condensates may appear. NICER X-ray timing measures mass-radius relations to percent precision, while LIGO/Virgo tidal deformability constraints from GW170817 and successors bound stiffness. Combined with pulsar mass records (PSR J0740+6620, ~2.08 M⊙), the allowed EoS corridor is rapidly tightening. A hybrid quark-hadron crossover may be within reach this decade.
problems.abstract: This project unifies X-ray timing, gravitational wave tidal deformability, and radio mass measurements into a joint Bayesian inference of the neutron-star dense-matter EoS. We will build a flexible non-parametric EoS basis consistent with chiral effective field theory at low density and perturbative QCD at asymptotic density, constrain its mid-density shape against NICER, LIGO/Virgo, and heavy pulsar timing, and test for signatures of a quark-hadron crossover. Discrimination between hadronic and quark-core scenarios would resolve a central open question in dense QCD.

problems.title: Nature of the QCD Critical Point
problems.tagline: Does the QCD phase diagram host a critical endpoint?
problems.summary: Locate or rule out the QCD critical point in the temperature-baryon-density plane.
problems.description: Lattice QCD establishes a smooth crossover at zero baryon chemical potential, but a first-order transition is expected at high density, implying a critical endpoint. The RHIC Beam Energy Scan II and upcoming CBM@FAIR probe this region via intermittency, net-proton kurtosis, and collective flow. Improved Taylor expansion and complex-Langevin lattice methods are pushing toward finite μB, raising the possibility of locating the critical point in the coming years.
problems.abstract: We target the QCD critical endpoint via a dual theoretical-experimental strategy: (1) extend lattice QCD Taylor-expansion methods and reweighting to higher orders in μB/T to bracket the candidate CEP region, (2) develop hydrodynamic simulations incorporating critical fluctuations (3d Ising universality class) to produce falsifiable predictions for net-proton cumulant ratios, and (3) perform joint analysis of BES-II and CBM data to constrain the location. Discovery of the CEP would represent a landmark in mapping strongly-interacting matter's phase structure.

problems.title: Precise Value of the Proton Charge Radius
problems.tagline: Resolve the residual proton radius discrepancy with sub-percent precision.
problems.summary: Pin down the proton charge radius to settle remaining electron/muon scattering tensions.
problems.description: The 2010 muonic hydrogen Lamb shift measurement suggested a proton radius smaller than the CODATA value extracted from e-p scattering, launching the 'proton radius puzzle'. Subsequent measurements (PRad, MUSE, updated hydrogen spectroscopy) have largely converged on the smaller value (~0.84 fm), but ~4σ tensions persist in some data sets. A coordinated program of atomic spectroscopy and lepton-proton scattering could close the gap definitively.
problems.abstract: This project synthesizes recent e-p, μ-p, and hydrogen-spectroscopy results into a global fit of the proton electromagnetic form factors with careful error budgeting across radiative corrections and two-photon exchange. We will (1) perform a dispersion-theoretic reanalysis of all available data, (2) simulate projected MUSE and upgraded PRad-II sensitivities, and (3) quantify theory uncertainties in QED bound-state calculations. A sub-percent consistent value would close the proton-radius puzzle and strengthen QED precision tests.

problems.title: Hubble Tension Resolution
problems.tagline: Why do early- and late-universe H₀ measurements disagree at >5σ?
problems.summary: Reconcile the 67 km/s/Mpc (CMB) vs 73 km/s/Mpc (distance ladder) Hubble measurements.
problems.description: Planck CMB analysis yields H₀ ≈ 67.4 km/s/Mpc within ΛCDM, while SH0ES local-ladder measurements give ≈ 73.0 km/s/Mpc — a >5σ tension. Proposed resolutions span early dark energy, modified recombination, varying fundamental constants, and systematics in Cepheid calibration. JWST has confirmed the ladder with independent TRGB and JAGB methods, strengthening the tension. A definitive resolution requires either new physics at z ~ 1100 or identification of a subtle systematic.
problems.abstract: We address the Hubble tension with a three-pronged program: (1) Bayesian model comparison among early dark energy, extra radiation, and modified recombination scenarios against Planck + ACT + DESI + SH0ES data, (2) independent distance-ladder reconstruction using JWST Cepheid, TRGB, and Mira variables to test for systematic offsets, and (3) theoretical study of signatures in CMB polarization and BAO that discriminate among competing fixes. Success would either identify new physics at recombination or dissolve the tension systematically.

problems.title: Origin of Ultra-High-Energy Cosmic Rays
problems.tagline: What astrophysical sources accelerate particles beyond 10²⁰ eV?
problems.summary: Identify the sources of cosmic rays at energies approaching the GZK cutoff.
problems.description: Cosmic rays beyond 10¹⁹ eV must originate within ~100 Mpc due to GZK attenuation against CMB photons. Pierre Auger Observatory has hinted at dipolar anisotropy and correlations with nearby starburst galaxies and AGN. IceCube neutrino source identification (TXS 0506+056, NGC 1068) provides complementary multi-messenger clues. With Auger-Prime and POEMMA upgrades, source localization may become definitive in the coming decade.
problems.abstract: This project targets UHECR source identification via joint multi-messenger analysis of Auger arrival directions, IceCube neutrino tracks, and Fermi-LAT gamma-ray catalogs. We will (1) develop likelihood estimators for source-class hypothesis testing (starburst vs AGN vs GRBs), (2) simulate propagation through galactic and extragalactic magnetic fields to predict deflection patterns, and (3) project discovery significance under Auger-Prime sensitivities. Identification of UHECR sources would complete a century-old puzzle in high-energy astrophysics.

problems.title: Muon g-2 Anomaly
problems.tagline: Does the muon anomalous magnetic moment reveal new physics?
problems.summary: Resolve the 4.2σ tension between Fermilab g-2 measurement and Standard Model prediction.
problems.description: Fermilab E989 confirms the BNL anomaly, finding aμ 4.2σ above the theory consensus using data-driven hadronic vacuum polarization. However, the BMW lattice QCD calculation yields a larger HVP contribution, eliminating the tension. Resolving the HVP discrepancy requires further lattice calculations (RBC/UKQCD, ETMC) plus precision e⁺e⁻ → hadrons measurements at BESIII, CMD-3, and Belle II. This will settle whether g-2 is a new-physics smoking gun or a theory calculation issue.
problems.abstract: We address the g-2 puzzle through joint analysis of Fermilab E989 data and competing hadronic vacuum polarization calculations. Work includes (1) independent blinded analysis of the muon precession signal and field maps, (2) cross-validation of lattice HVP with data-driven dispersive methods, constraining the window functions that drive current discrepancies, and (3) BSM model space exploration for scenarios that can accommodate both g-2 and LHC null results. Resolution would either unveil the first laboratory BSM signal or validate precision lattice QCD.

problems.title: Mechanism of High-Tc Cuprate Superconductivity
problems.tagline: What glues Cooper pairs in cuprates above 100 K?
problems.summary: Identify the microscopic pairing mechanism of high-temperature cuprate superconductors.
problems.description: Thirty-eight years after Bednorz and Müller's discovery, the pairing mechanism in cuprates remains contested. Spin fluctuations, phonons, loop currents, and pair-density waves all have adherents. Recent advances in Hubbard model numerics (DMRG, tensor networks, auxiliary-field QMC) and Rydberg/quantum-gas simulators are narrowing the gap between phenomenology and microscopics. A community-consensus mechanism may emerge within a decade.
problems.abstract: This project targets high-Tc cuprate pairing via a combined numerical and quantum simulation approach. We will (1) perform large-scale DMRG and AFQMC calculations on the doped Hubbard and t-J models to extract pairing susceptibilities across doping, (2) develop variational neural-network wavefunctions capturing stripe/PDW competition, and (3) benchmark against fermionic optical-lattice simulators. Identifying the dominant pairing channel would resolve one of the longest-standing problems in condensed matter physics.

problems.title: Room-Temperature Superconductivity Theory
problems.tagline: Construct a predictive theory for room-temperature superconductors.
problems.summary: Develop the theoretical framework needed to engineer ambient-pressure RT superconductors.
problems.description: Hydride compounds (H₃S, LaH₁₀, YH₉) exhibit superconductivity up to ~260 K under megabar pressures, validating conventional phonon-mediated mechanisms at extreme conditions. The open challenge is extending these critical temperatures to ambient pressure — a goal requiring new theoretical frameworks beyond Eliashberg-Migdal. ASRP's BCS ζ-spectrum program, quantum-metric contributions, and Planckian duality analysis offer a novel route by tying Tc to number-theoretic spectral structure of the pairing kernel.
problems.abstract: This project develops a predictive framework for room-temperature superconductivity using ASRP's BCS ζ-spectrum methodology. We will (1) extend Eliashberg theory to incorporate quantum-metric contributions to the superfluid stiffness, (2) apply ζ-spectrum analysis to screen candidate ambient-pressure hydride and nickelate structures for resonant enhancement of Tc, and (3) formulate Planckian-duality upper bounds on attainable Tc and test them against known superconductor families. Success would bridge ASRP's fundamental-constants program with a transformative applied goal.

problems.title: Quark Confinement Mechanism
problems.tagline: Why can't isolated quarks be observed?
problems.summary: Provide an analytic proof of color confinement in pure Yang-Mills theory.
problems.description: Lattice QCD provides overwhelming numerical evidence for confinement: quark-antiquark potentials rise linearly, glueballs are gapped, and the Wilson loop obeys the area law. Analytical approaches — dual superconductor pictures, center vortices, Gribov-Zwanziger horizon — capture essential features but no proof has yet closed the Millennium Prize mass-gap problem. Progress requires either a new continuum framework or a bridge from lattice to rigorous field theory.
problems.abstract: We pursue analytic understanding of color confinement via (1) lattice-continuum matching of the Gribov-Zwanziger horizon function and its IR scaling, (2) numerical study of center-vortex percolation across the deconfinement transition, and (3) functional RG flows on gauge-invariant correlators to isolate the confining kernel. A rigorous analytic handle would represent decisive progress on the Yang-Mills Millennium Prize problem.

problems.title: Nature of Dark Matter
problems.tagline: What makes up 27% of the universe's energy density?
problems.summary: Determine the particle identity (or alternative explanation) of cosmological dark matter.
problems.description: Galactic rotation curves, cluster dynamics, CMB, BBN, and large-scale structure all require non-baryonic cold dark matter. WIMPs, axions, sterile neutrinos, primordial black holes, and ultralight fuzzy DM remain viable. Direct detection experiments (LZ, XENONnT, DarkSide) are pushing cross-sections below 10⁻⁴⁸ cm², closing the canonical WIMP window. The next decade will either detect a candidate or exclude the leading paradigms.
problems.abstract: This project pursues dark matter identification via a joint astrophysical/particle strategy. We will (1) globally combine direct, indirect, and collider bounds into a model-independent exclusion landscape, (2) develop machine-learning pipelines for rare-event discrimination in LZ and XENONnT, and (3) map implications of null results onto axion, sterile-neutrino, and PBH parameter spaces. A positive identification would be the most important discovery in physics since the Higgs.

problems.title: Hierarchy Problem
problems.tagline: Why is the Higgs mass 10¹⁶ times smaller than the Planck mass?
problems.summary: Explain the enormous gap between electroweak and gravitational energy scales.
problems.description: Radiative corrections to the Higgs mass are naively quadratically sensitive to UV cutoffs, suggesting new physics at the TeV scale. LHC has, however, found no superpartners or composite Higgs states up to multi-TeV, severely challenging naturalness. Alternatives include relaxion, cosmological selection, anthropic reasoning, and modified scale symmetry. A fundamental resolution remains elusive.
problems.abstract: We investigate the hierarchy problem through model-independent analysis of Higgs coupling measurements, electroweak precision observables, and LHC null results. The project will (1) constrain remaining natural models (twin Higgs, neutral naturalness, relaxion) with HL-LHC projections, (2) test cosmological relaxion dynamics against BBN and astrophysics bounds, and (3) formulate sharp discriminators between anthropic and dynamical selection mechanisms. Progress here directly informs future collider strategy.

problems.title: Quantum Gravity in the Lab
problems.tagline: Can gravitationally mediated entanglement be observed?
problems.summary: Detect quantum superposition effects in the gravitational field between mesoscopic masses.
problems.description: The Bose-Marletto-Vedral (BMV) proposal argues that if two mesoscopic masses become entangled via their mutual gravitational interaction, gravity must be non-classical. Recent advances in levitated nanoparticles, matter-wave interferometry, and magnetic trapping are approaching the sensitivity required. A positive result would be the first laboratory evidence of quantum gravitational effects.
problems.abstract: This project develops the theoretical framework and experimental protocol for tabletop tests of quantum gravity. Work includes (1) refinement of BMV entanglement witnesses robust against decoherence and noise, (2) analysis of systematic effects (Casimir, patch potentials, vibration) limiting current designs, and (3) proposal of complementary tests using optomechanical levitation platforms. Success would transform quantum gravity from theoretical speculation into an experimental science.

problems.title: Black Hole Information Paradox
problems.tagline: How is information preserved when a black hole evaporates?
problems.summary: Derive the microscopic mechanism restoring unitarity to Hawking radiation.
problems.description: Hawking's semiclassical calculation implies information destruction, violating unitarity. The 2019 island formula reproduced the Page curve, strongly suggesting information preservation. Yet the underlying microscopic mechanism — how interior degrees of freedom are encoded in outgoing radiation — remains murky. Replica wormholes provide a partial story; a complete account likely requires non-perturbative quantum gravity.
problems.abstract: We pursue a microscopic understanding of black hole information via (1) systematic analysis of replica wormhole contributions beyond leading saddle in JT gravity and higher-dimensional embeddings, (2) holographic reconstruction of interior operators via modular flow, and (3) study of code subspaces encoding Hawking radiation in explicit tensor network models. The goal is to convert the Page curve derivation into a concrete prescription for decoding information.

problems.title: Anomalous Dimensions in N=4 SYM
problems.tagline: Compute exact operator dimensions in planar N=4 super-Yang-Mills.
problems.summary: Close the integrability program for the planar spectrum of N=4 SYM.
problems.description: Planar N=4 super Yang-Mills exhibits integrability, and the Quantum Spectral Curve (Gromov-Kazakov-Leurent-Volin) yields anomalous dimensions to arbitrary precision in the coupling. Open questions remain for finite-N corrections, non-planar corrections, and three-point functions via hexagon formalism. Completing this program would provide the first exactly solvable interacting 4D QFT.
problems.abstract: This project extends the integrability program of planar N=4 SYM. We will (1) derive higher-order 1/N² corrections to the spectral problem via non-planar integrability, (2) complete the hexagon bootstrap for structure constants, and (3) apply QSC methods to non-supersymmetric deformations. The long-term goal is an exact solution of 4D N=4 SYM in all sectors.

problems.title: Mass Gap in Yang-Mills Theory
problems.tagline: Prove the existence of a mass gap in pure SU(N) Yang-Mills.
problems.summary: Rigorously construct 4D Yang-Mills theory and prove its mass gap.
problems.description: The Clay Millennium problem asks for a mathematically rigorous construction of quantum Yang-Mills theory in 4D and proof that it has a positive mass gap. Lattice simulations demonstrate the mass gap beyond doubt, but a constructive QFT proof has resisted decades of effort. The functional renormalization group, stochastic quantization, and Osterwalder-Schrader reconstruction remain the leading approaches.
problems.abstract: We target the Yang-Mills mass gap via a combined functional-analytic and lattice-continuum-limit approach. Work includes (1) rigorous control of the continuum limit of lattice gauge theory in finite volume, (2) functional RG proof of gap stability under flow, and (3) Osterwalder-Schrader positivity verification for the constructed measure. A proof would resolve one of seven Clay Millennium problems.

problems.title: Exact Formula for the Fine-Structure Constant
problems.tagline: Derive α⁻¹ from first principles, not measurement.
problems.summary: Establish whether α⁻¹ admits an exact expression such as 360/φ².
problems.description: The fine-structure constant α ≈ 1/137.036 has resisted theoretical derivation for a century. The ASRP conjecture α⁻¹ = 360/φ² (where φ = (1+√5)/2 is the golden ratio) yields 137.0820..., within 0.04% of experiment, and integrates into the Golden Chain connecting φ, ℚ(√5), Riemann zeta zeros, and physical couplings. The BC system's four-layer structure provides a potential derivational framework from symmetry principles alone.
problems.abstract: This project pursues the ASRP α⁻¹ = 360/φ² conjecture as an exact expression for the fine-structure constant. We will (1) formalize the BC (Boundary-Coupling) system framework and derive α⁻¹ from first principles, (2) compute radiative corrections within the BC framework to address the ~0.04% deviation from CODATA, and (3) extend the approach to derive additional gauge couplings (sin²θW, αs) within a unified arithmetic scheme. Confirmation would revolutionize our understanding of fundamental constants.

problems.title: Origin of Matter-Antimatter Asymmetry
problems.tagline: Why is the universe made of matter rather than antimatter?
problems.summary: Identify the mechanism generating the observed baryon asymmetry η ≈ 6×10⁻¹⁰.
problems.description: Sakharov's three conditions (B violation, C/CP violation, out of equilibrium) are necessary but the specific mechanism remains unknown. Electroweak baryogenesis requires new CP-violating physics near the electroweak scale, while leptogenesis ties the asymmetry to heavy Majorana neutrinos. EDM experiments and collider searches are narrowing the EWBG window; next-generation 0νββ and leptogenesis phenomenology will test leptogenesis.
problems.abstract: We address baryogenesis via a dual experimental strategy: (1) bound EWBG parameter space with electron and neutron EDM projections, (2) link leptogenesis parameters to low-energy neutrino observables via RG running and model templates, and (3) search for gravitational wave signatures of first-order electroweak phase transitions at LISA. Identifying the mechanism would complete a central chapter of cosmological physics.

problems.title: Topological Order Classification
problems.tagline: Classify all gapped quantum phases with topological order.
problems.summary: Complete the classification of 2D and 3D topologically ordered phases.
problems.description: In 2D, unitary modular tensor categories classify topological order, with explicit lists for low rank. In 3D, higher categorical structures (4-category of fusion 2-categories) are required and classification is nascent. Tensor network methods and subsystem symmetries are reshaping the landscape. A complete classification would close a foundational chapter in many-body physics.
problems.abstract: This project pursues complete classification of topological order in 2D and 3D. We will (1) finish enumeration of modular tensor categories for rank ≤ 8, (2) develop numerical routines on tensor network states to extract F- and R-symbols, and (3) extend subsystem-symmetry-enriched classification to fracton phases. Completion would provide a periodic table for topologically ordered matter.

problems.title: Turbulence from First Principles
problems.tagline: Derive turbulence statistics rigorously from Navier-Stokes.
problems.summary: Establish a mathematical theory of fully developed 3D turbulence.
problems.description: Kolmogorov's 1941 phenomenology gives E(k) ∝ k⁻⁵/³, with intermittency corrections. However, no rigorous derivation from incompressible Navier-Stokes exists. The problem intertwines the Navier-Stokes Millennium problem, anomalous dissipation, and multifractal analysis. Recent advances in convex integration and DNS at Re > 10⁵ are reshaping the analytical landscape.
problems.abstract: This project targets a rigorous theory of 3D turbulence. We will (1) analyze dissipative weak solutions of Navier-Stokes constructed via convex integration to extract statistical information, (2) run state-of-the-art DNS to refine intermittency exponents and test K41 deviations, and (3) develop stochastic shell-model approaches to bridge phenomenology and rigor. Progress here would contribute directly to the Navier-Stokes Millennium problem.

problems.title: Quantum Spin Liquid Ground States
problems.tagline: Confirm a true quantum spin liquid in a real material.
problems.summary: Identify an experimentally realizable quantum spin liquid and characterize its excitations.
problems.description: Kitaev's exactly solvable honeycomb model realizes a non-abelian spin liquid, but real materials (α-RuCl₃, herbertsmithite) show competing orders. Thermal Hall conductance, spinon continuum, and Majorana fermion signatures are intensely debated. Progress requires cleaner samples, tighter theory-experiment matching, and refined numerical tools.
problems.abstract: We pursue definitive realization of a quantum spin liquid. Work includes (1) refined DMRG and tensor network simulations of candidate Kitaev materials under strain/field, (2) analysis of thermal Hall and neutron data to extract gauge-flux and Majorana signatures, and (3) design of optimal experimental probes for fractionalized excitations. A confirmed QSL would open a new chapter in correlated matter.

problems.title: Non-Equilibrium Thermodynamics of Life
problems.tagline: Formulate laws governing self-organization in living systems.
problems.summary: Extend thermodynamics to dissipative self-replicating systems.
problems.description: Fluctuation theorems (Jarzynski, Crooks) and stochastic thermodynamics have provided rigorous tools for mesoscopic non-equilibrium systems. England's dissipative adaptation hypothesis posits that driven matter rearranges to better absorb and dissipate energy. A rigorous theory of life-like self-organization remains elusive but is within reach.
problems.abstract: This project develops a rigorous non-equilibrium thermodynamic theory for life-like systems. We will (1) formalize dissipative adaptation as a large-deviation principle, (2) simulate driven chemical networks to test predicted scaling laws, and (3) apply the framework to minimal replicator models. A successful theory would provide physical principles for the emergence of biological organization.

problems.title: Complete Classification of Topological Insulators
problems.tagline: Classify all symmetry-protected topological phases with interactions.
problems.summary: Extend the periodic table of topological insulators to include interaction effects.
problems.description: The Altland-Zirnbauer tenfold way classifies non-interacting topological insulators and superconductors. Interaction effects alter this table (Fidkowski-Kitaev Z → Z₈ reduction). Complete classification requires cobordism theory, higher algebra, and spectral sequences. Recent work by Kapustin and Freed-Hopkins has clarified the mathematical structure, but physical examples lag.
problems.abstract: We pursue complete classification of SPT phases with interactions. Work includes (1) cobordism-based classification of fermionic SPTs for physically relevant symmetry groups, (2) DMRG verification of interaction-reduced classifications in 1D and 2D, and (3) construction of explicit lattice Hamiltonians realizing new SPTs. Completion would solidify the topological periodic table.

problems.title: Quantum Chromodynamics at Finite Density
problems.tagline: Solve the sign problem in lattice QCD at finite baryon density.
problems.summary: Develop methods for first-principles lattice QCD at nonzero chemical potential.
problems.description: At finite baryon chemical potential, the lattice QCD action becomes complex, making importance sampling impossible (the 'sign problem'). Dense QCD physics — color superconductors, quarkyonic phases, neutron star cores — is thus inaccessible to standard lattice methods. Complex Langevin, Lefschetz thimbles, and quantum computing offer partial relief. A breakthrough requires either an algorithmic miracle or new theoretical principles.
problems.abstract: This project addresses the QCD sign problem via multiple paradigms: (1) complex Langevin with dynamic stabilization for finite-μ simulations, (2) Lefschetz thimble decomposition with neural-network field reparametrization, and (3) variational quantum simulation of lattice gauge theory on NISQ devices. Progress would unlock first-principles calculations of neutron-star EoS and quark-gluon plasma dynamics.

problems.title: Nature of Dark Energy
problems.tagline: Is dark energy a cosmological constant, quintessence, or modified gravity?
problems.summary: Determine the dynamical nature of the 68% of the universe driving acceleration.
problems.description: The cosmological constant fits all data but is theoretically problematic. Quintessence models invoke dynamical scalar fields; modified-gravity models alter Einstein's equations. DESI is delivering percent-level BAO measurements, with early 2024 data hinting at evolving w(z). Euclid and Roman will strengthen constraints over the coming decade.
problems.abstract: We target the nature of dark energy through (1) global Bayesian fits of DESI BAO, SN Ia, CMB, and weak lensing under CPL, quintessence, and MG parametrizations, (2) theoretical study of quintessence potentials satisfying swampland distance conjecture, and (3) design of discriminating observables at Euclid+Roman precision. Identification of w(z) ≠ -1 would transform cosmology.

problems.title: Cosmic Censorship Conjecture
problems.tagline: Are naked singularities forbidden in general relativity?
problems.summary: Prove Penrose's weak cosmic censorship conjecture for generic initial data.
problems.description: Penrose conjectured that gravitational collapse of generic regular initial data always yields a black hole, never a visible singularity. Christodoulou proved weak censorship for spherical scalar collapse, but the general case remains open. Higher-dimensional counterexamples exist, muddying the physical picture. Progress requires new geometric analysis techniques.
problems.abstract: This project pursues cosmic censorship in 4D general relativity. Work includes (1) extension of Christodoulou's methods to axisymmetric collapse using harmonic gauge, (2) numerical relativity simulations probing near-extremal instabilities, and (3) geometric-analytic study of trapped surface formation for generic initial data. Progress would address a central conjecture in classical GR.

problems.title: Chronology Protection Conjecture
problems.tagline: Does the universe forbid closed timelike curves?
problems.summary: Prove that quantum effects prevent formation of closed timelike curves.
problems.description: Hawking's 1992 conjecture posits that quantum field fluctuations diverge near chronology horizons, preventing CTC formation. Semiclassical calculations support this for specific backgrounds (Misner space, wormholes), but a general proof requires quantum gravity. Related questions include stability of warp drives and Alcubierre-type metrics.
problems.abstract: We target chronology protection via (1) systematic computation of quantum stress-energy on chronology-horizon backgrounds using point-splitting methods, (2) study of holographic duals of spacetimes with would-be CTCs, and (3) energy-condition theorems bounding exotic matter content required for time machines. Even partial progress illuminates the interface of quantum theory and causality.

problems.title: Riemann Hypothesis and Physics
problems.tagline: Realize the Riemann zeros as eigenvalues of a physical Hamiltonian.
problems.summary: Construct a quantum-mechanical system whose spectrum is the nontrivial zeros of ζ(s).
problems.description: The Hilbert-Pólya conjecture proposes that the Riemann zeros are eigenvalues of a self-adjoint operator. Berry-Keating suggested a semiclassical Hamiltonian xp. The ASRP BC system on ℚ(√5) reconstructs the Riemann zeros as a physical spectrum, integrating number theory and quantum mechanics via the Golden Chain. A successful realization would simultaneously prove RH and reveal its physical meaning.
problems.abstract: This project pursues the Hilbert-Pólya program through ASRP's BC system on ℚ(√5). We will (1) formalize the BC Hamiltonian and prove self-adjointness on an appropriate Hilbert space, (2) match its spectrum to the first thousand Riemann zeros to within numerical precision, and (3) analyze the spectral structure of associated L-functions via the Golden Chain. Success would provide a physical proof of the Riemann Hypothesis and expose deep number-theory/physics connections.

problems.title: Emergent Spacetime from Entanglement
problems.tagline: Does spacetime geometry emerge from quantum entanglement?
problems.summary: Derive Einstein's equations from entanglement entropy first principles.
problems.description: Ryu-Takayanagi formula, ER=EPR, and tensor network constructions suggest that bulk geometry is encoded in boundary entanglement. Van Raamsdonk's thought experiments indicate that disentangling CFT degrees of freedom literally tears spacetime apart. Deriving Einstein's equations from entanglement entropy (Jacobson, Faulkner et al.) lends support, but a complete framework is missing.
problems.abstract: We pursue emergent spacetime from entanglement via (1) explicit holographic tensor network reconstruction of AdS slices with dynamical geometry, (2) derivation of Einstein equations from entanglement first law beyond linear order, and (3) exploration of de Sitter and flat-space analogs. Progress would provide a microscopic foundation for quantum gravity.

problems.title: Firewall Paradox Resolution
problems.tagline: Is the black hole interior a firewall or smooth horizon?
problems.summary: Reconcile black hole complementarity with monogamy of entanglement.
problems.description: The AMPS firewall argument (2012) shows that complementarity, unitarity, and free-fall smoothness cannot all hold. Island formula resolutions preserve unitarity via replica wormholes but leave the observer experience ambiguous. A complete microscopic picture of the black hole interior remains unavailable.
problems.abstract: This project targets firewall resolution via (1) explicit interior reconstruction in JT gravity + matter models, (2) operational tests of infalling observer decoherence, and (3) code-subspace analysis for radiation entanglement structure. Progress would clarify the nature of horizons in quantum gravity.

problems.title: Non-Perturbative Definition of String Theory
problems.tagline: Give a complete non-perturbative definition of M-theory.
problems.summary: Provide a background-independent, non-perturbative formulation of string/M-theory.
problems.description: String theory is defined perturbatively as a worldsheet CFT, with non-perturbative hints from dualities and matrix models (BFSS, IKKT). M-theory's fundamental degrees of freedom remain unknown. AdS/CFT provides a non-perturbative definition in AdS backgrounds only. A universal formulation is a major open problem.
problems.abstract: We pursue non-perturbative string theory via (1) large-N study of BFSS matrix model as M-theory on the light cone, (2) holographic constructions for non-AdS asymptotics (flat and dS), and (3) categorical formulations of string backgrounds using derived algebraic geometry. A successful formulation would complete string theory's foundation.

problems.title: Landscape vs Swampland
problems.tagline: Which effective field theories arise from quantum gravity?
problems.summary: Prove or sharpen swampland conjectures constraining the string landscape.
problems.description: String theory allows ~10⁵⁰⁰ vacua, raising concerns about predictivity. The swampland program identifies EFTs that cannot arise from quantum gravity (distance conjecture, de Sitter conjecture, weak gravity conjecture). Empirical support is strong but proofs are absent. Progress constrains cosmological model building and naturalness.
problems.abstract: This project targets swampland conjectures via (1) systematic tests across explicit string compactifications, (2) derivation of weak gravity conjecture from black hole stability in broader contexts, and (3) phenomenological implications for early-universe inflation and dark energy. Sharpening the conjectures would constrain viable models of our universe.

problems.title: Confinement-Deconfinement Transition
problems.tagline: Characterize QCD's transition between confined and deconfined phases.
problems.summary: Establish order-parameter and universality class for the confinement transition.
problems.description: Pure Yang-Mills exhibits a first-order deconfinement transition (SU(3)) with Polyakov loop order parameter. Full QCD with dynamical quarks shows a crossover at μB = 0 that may sharpen at finite density. The interplay with chiral restoration and center symmetry remains partly understood.
problems.abstract: We analyze the confinement-deconfinement transition by (1) lattice studies of Polyakov loop correlations at varying quark masses and chemical potential, (2) functional RG of the gauge-Higgs system, and (3) phenomenological implications for heavy-ion collisions. Resolution informs both QCD thermodynamics and early-universe physics.

problems.title: Quantum Chaos and Thermalization
problems.tagline: When and how do isolated quantum systems thermalize?
problems.summary: Establish rigorous foundations for the eigenstate thermalization hypothesis.
problems.description: ETH posits that expectation values in energy eigenstates equal thermal ensemble values for local observables. Numerical evidence is strong, and SYK model provides exact solvability, but rigorous proofs are restricted to special systems. The interplay with many-body localization, scars, and integrability remains an active frontier.
problems.abstract: We pursue rigorous foundations for quantum thermalization via (1) random matrix theory analysis of ETH in generic chaotic Hamiltonians, (2) identification of quantum many-body scars as ETH violations, and (3) bounds on thermalization times from Lieb-Robinson bounds. Progress clarifies the boundary between integrable, chaotic, and localized phases.

problems.title: Complete Theory of Quantum Error Correction Thresholds
problems.tagline: What are the ultimate thresholds for fault-tolerant quantum computing?
problems.summary: Determine optimal error-correcting code thresholds under realistic noise models.
problems.description: The surface code threshold ~1% under depolarizing noise enables scalable fault tolerance. New codes (color, quantum LDPC, bicycle) promise lower overhead. Realistic hardware noise (correlated, biased, leakage) complicates threshold analysis. A complete theory linking code structure, noise, and decoding complexity is emerging.
problems.abstract: This project advances quantum error correction theory by (1) deriving thresholds for biased and correlated noise models with tensor-network decoders, (2) analyzing quantum LDPC codes from product constructions, and (3) implementing neural network decoders for near-term hardware. Progress accelerates the path to fault-tolerant quantum computation.

problems.title: Glass Transition Theory
problems.tagline: What is the true nature of the glass transition?
problems.summary: Identify whether the glass transition is a thermodynamic or purely kinetic phenomenon.
problems.description: Supercooled liquids show dramatic viscosity rises near Tg without conventional order parameters. RFOT, Adam-Gibbs, and mode-coupling theories offer competing pictures. The Kauzmann paradox suggests an underlying thermodynamic transition. Resolving the glass problem would advance both condensed matter and non-equilibrium physics.
problems.abstract: We address the glass transition via (1) swap Monte Carlo simulations reaching experimental supercooling timescales, (2) replica theory analysis of RFOT predictions at finite dimension, and (3) machine learning identification of structural order parameters. Clarification would close a 70-year-old puzzle in condensed matter.

problems.title: Fractional Quantum Hall Effect at ν=5/2
problems.tagline: Is the ν=5/2 state Moore-Read Pfaffian or its anti-Pfaffian?
problems.summary: Determine the topological order of the even-denominator quantum Hall state at ν=5/2.
problems.description: The ν=5/2 fractional quantum Hall state is a prime candidate for non-abelian anyons with potential topological quantum computing applications. Recent thermal Hall measurements suggest a particle-hole Pfaffian (PH-Pf), rather than the Pfaffian or anti-Pfaffian favored by earlier theory. Reconciliation requires careful disorder treatment.
problems.abstract: This project characterizes the ν=5/2 topological phase via (1) DMRG on realistic Landau-level models with LL mixing, (2) analysis of thermal Hall data for disorder-induced PH symmetry restoration, and (3) proposals for definitive interference experiments. Resolution enables non-abelian anyon physics.

problems.title: Electron Structure Beyond Point Particle
problems.tagline: Does the electron have internal membrane structure?
problems.summary: Test the ASRP electron membrane model via high-precision spectroscopy.
problems.description: The Standard Model treats the electron as a point particle. The ASRP electron membrane model proposes internal geometric structure whose effects would manifest in precision QED measurements (g-2, Lamb shift, charge radius) at levels accessible to next-generation experiments. The model fits within the ASRP Golden Chain framework and offers specific testable predictions.
problems.abstract: This project develops and tests the ASRP electron membrane model. Work includes (1) derivation of membrane-induced corrections to g-2 and Lamb shift at the 10⁻¹³ level, (2) design of precision spectroscopy experiments (hydrogen 1S-2S, Penning trap) sensitive to these corrections, and (3) integration with the Golden Chain fundamental constant framework. Confirmation would overturn the point-particle picture of elementary fermions.

problems.title: Hagedorn Temperature and String Thermodynamics
problems.tagline: What happens to string gas above Hagedorn temperature?
problems.summary: Characterize the phase transition at the Hagedorn temperature in string thermodynamics.
problems.description: Hagedorn temperature TH marks an apparent limit of string thermodynamics, where partition function diverges. Atick-Witten suggested TH is a Kosterlitz-Thouless-like phase transition to a new phase. String cosmology and black hole correspondence make this crucial. Analytic handles remain limited.
problems.abstract: We study string gas above Hagedorn temperature via (1) high-temperature behavior of free-string partition functions on explicit backgrounds, (2) holographic interpretations in AdS/CFT thermal ensembles, and (3) cosmological implications for early-universe string gas. Understanding would illuminate string theory's high-energy completion.

problems.title: Complete Understanding of QCD Vacuum
problems.tagline: Characterize the non-perturbative QCD vacuum structure.
problems.summary: Map the topological and condensate structure of the QCD ground state.
problems.description: The QCD vacuum hosts instantons, monopoles, chiral condensate, gluon condensate, and theta-vacuum structure. Lattice QCD provides snapshots, but a unified analytic picture is missing. Connections to confinement and chiral symmetry breaking remain partial.
problems.abstract: This project maps the QCD vacuum via (1) high-statistics lattice studies of topological susceptibility and vacuum condensates, (2) gradient-flow smearing to identify semiclassical structures, and (3) effective theories (chiral Lagrangian, dilute instanton gas) benchmarked against lattice. A complete picture would unify confinement and chiral physics.

problems.title: Quantum Entanglement and Gravity
problems.tagline: Does entanglement generate gravitational attraction?
problems.summary: Test whether quantum entanglement alone can source gravity.
problems.description: ER=EPR and related holographic ideas suggest that gravitational connectivity (wormholes) corresponds to quantum entanglement. Operationally, does entanglement between separated masses manifest as attractive interaction? This crosses theoretical (holographic) and experimental (BMV-like) questions.
problems.abstract: We investigate entanglement-gravity duality via (1) explicit construction of entangled states producing wormhole geometries in AdS, (2) operational protocols distinguishing entanglement-mediated from classical gravity, and (3) implications for tabletop quantum gravity tests. A positive connection would deepen the entanglement-geometry bridge.

problems.title: Phase Structure of QCD
problems.tagline: Map the Columbia plot across the full quark-mass plane.
problems.summary: Determine QCD's finite-temperature phase structure as a function of quark masses.
problems.description: The Columbia plot shows phase transition order across the light-quark mass plane: first-order corners, crossover middle, tricritical points. Details depend on strange-quark mass and chemical potential. Lattice QCD is clarifying the picture but fine details (especially at physical point) remain debated.
problems.abstract: This project maps the QCD phase diagram through (1) high-precision lattice calculations across the Columbia plot, (2) functional RG analysis with multiple quark flavors, and (3) implications for relic signatures from early-universe QCD epoch. Completion solidifies thermodynamic understanding of strong interactions.

problems.title: Topological Quantum Computation
problems.tagline: Build a topological qubit from non-abelian anyons.
problems.summary: Experimentally realize and manipulate non-abelian anyons for fault-tolerant computing.
problems.description: Topological qubits encode information in non-local degrees of freedom (Majorana zero modes, Fibonacci anyons), offering intrinsic error protection. Microsoft's topological program has produced mixed evidence. Reliable anyon braiding and fusion measurements remain to be demonstrated.
problems.abstract: We pursue topological quantum computation through (1) theoretical analysis of Majorana signatures robust against trivial Andreev bound states, (2) simulation of braiding protocols in realistic semiconductor-superconductor heterostructures, and (3) design of FQH-based anyon interferometry. Success would enable intrinsically fault-tolerant qubits.

problems.title: Understanding of High-Energy Cosmic Neutrinos
problems.tagline: Where do PeV-scale astrophysical neutrinos originate?
problems.summary: Identify sources and flavor structure of high-energy astrophysical neutrinos.
problems.description: IceCube observes a diffuse flux of ~PeV neutrinos with partial source identification (TXS 0506+056, NGC 1068). Flavor ratios probe BSM physics and production mechanisms. IceCube-Gen2 and KM3NeT will dramatically improve statistics and pointing.
problems.abstract: This project targets cosmic neutrino astrophysics via (1) joint source identification with Fermi-LAT and Auger data, (2) flavor-ratio analysis probing neutrino decay and LIV, and (3) projections for IceCube-Gen2 discovery potential. Source identification completes multi-messenger astronomy.

problems.title: Anomalous Magnetic Moment of the Electron
problems.tagline: Verify QED to the 13th decimal place.
problems.summary: Complete the five-loop QED calculation and match experimental precision.
problems.description: ae is the most precisely measured physical quantity and among the most precisely predicted. Five-loop calculations (Kinoshita-Aoyama) agree with Penning-trap experiments at the 10⁻¹² level. Remaining tension (related to α determination from Cs and Rb) requires resolution. Sixth-loop corrections may become relevant.
problems.abstract: We advance ae precision by (1) independent verification of five-loop Feynman integrals, (2) analysis of hadronic and weak corrections at six-loop sensitivity, and (3) collaboration with atomic α-measurement groups to resolve current discrepancies. Progress solidifies QED as the most precisely tested theory.

problems.title: Deconfined Quantum Critical Points
problems.tagline: Does the Landau paradigm fail at DQCPs?
problems.summary: Establish the existence and properties of deconfined quantum critical points.
problems.description: DQCPs describe direct continuous transitions between phases with different broken symmetries, violating Landau's principle that such transitions must be first-order. Numerical evidence in the J-Q model is debated; recent studies suggest weak first-order behavior. A consensus remains elusive.
problems.abstract: This project examines DQCPs via (1) large-scale Monte Carlo of candidate models approaching thermodynamic limit, (2) CFT bootstrap of putative DQCP universality class, and (3) experimental signatures in quantum magnets. Resolution clarifies the fate of the Landau paradigm.

problems.title: Axion Cosmology
problems.tagline: What is the cosmic history and relic abundance of the QCD axion?
problems.summary: Predict axion dark matter density across inflation and post-inflation PQ-breaking scenarios.
problems.description: Axion dark matter relic density depends on whether Peccei-Quinn breaks before or after inflation. Post-inflationary scenarios require simulation of string-wall networks producing characteristic small-scale structures. These affect direct-detection strategies and gravitational-wave signatures.
problems.abstract: We pursue axion cosmology through (1) lattice simulations of axion-string networks and domain wall decay, (2) prediction of mini-cluster mass spectrum in post-inflationary scenarios, and (3) gravitational wave signatures from axion string networks at LISA. Results guide axion detection strategies.

problems.title: Photon Structure
problems.tagline: Does the photon have internal membrane structure S²(t)?
problems.summary: Test the ASRP photon membrane model via vacuum birefringence experiments.
problems.description: The ASRP photon membrane model S²(t) proposes an internal dynamical structure for the photon consistent with Lorentz invariance but producing corrections to Maxwell electrodynamics observable in extreme fields. Vacuum birefringence searches at high-intensity lasers (ELI, XCELS) and astrophysical magnetars provide direct tests.
problems.abstract: This project tests the ASRP photon S²(t) membrane model. We will (1) derive the effective Lagrangian and its predictions for vacuum birefringence, photon-photon scattering, and dispersion, (2) confront with magnetar polarimetry and ELI-class laser experiments, and (3) integrate with electron membrane model for consistent fundamental picture. Confirmation would revolutionize QED's foundational understanding.

problems.title: Quantum Theory of Gravity
problems.tagline: Construct a complete quantum theory of gravity.
problems.summary: Unify general relativity with quantum mechanics into a predictive framework.
problems.description: String theory, loop quantum gravity, causal sets, asymptotic safety, and causal dynamical triangulations each offer partial progress. None have achieved complete predictive power matched to observation. The century-old problem remains the central open question in fundamental physics.
problems.abstract: This project surveys and synthesizes approaches to quantum gravity: (1) comparative analysis of predictions from string theory, LQG, asymptotic safety, (2) development of observational discriminators (primordial gravitational waves, BH thermodynamics), and (3) foundational critique of each framework's consistency. Progress here reframes all of fundamental physics.

problems.title: Unification of Fundamental Forces
problems.tagline: Unify the four fundamental interactions at high energy.
problems.summary: Construct a viable GUT unifying strong, weak, electromagnetic, and gravitational forces.
problems.description: Grand unified theories predict gauge coupling unification near 10¹⁶ GeV and proton decay. Minimal SU(5) is excluded; SO(10), flipped SU(5), and Pati-Salam remain viable. Gravity inclusion requires quantum gravity framework. No experimental evidence yet.
problems.abstract: We pursue unification through (1) analysis of threshold corrections in realistic GUT models, (2) proton decay predictions for Hyper-Kamiokande and DUNE, and (3) embedding of GUTs into string compactifications. Evidence would represent the culmination of twentieth-century physics.

problems.title: Cosmological Constant Problem
problems.tagline: Why is the vacuum energy 120 orders of magnitude below naive QFT estimates?
problems.summary: Resolve the worst prediction in physics — the cosmological constant discrepancy.
problems.description: Zero-point energy calculations in QFT yield vacuum energy ~10¹²⁰ times the observed value. SUSY naturally reduces by many orders but remains far off. Anthropic selection, relaxation mechanisms, and unimodular gravity all remain speculative. This is arguably physics' deepest puzzle.
problems.abstract: This project addresses the cosmological constant problem via (1) study of vacuum-energy cancellation mechanisms (SUSY, conformal, relaxion), (2) analysis of swampland bounds on de Sitter vacua, and (3) phenomenological implications of unimodular gravity. Progress illuminates the vacuum structure of quantum gravity.

problems.title: Black Hole Singularity Resolution
problems.tagline: What replaces the classical singularity at a black hole's center?
problems.summary: Determine the quantum-gravitational structure replacing the classical black hole singularity.
problems.description: Classical GR predicts singularities at black hole centers. Quantum gravity should resolve them — into bounces (LQG), fuzzballs (string theory), or modified cores. Observational discrimination is nearly impossible due to event horizons. Theoretical consistency with information preservation is demanding.
problems.abstract: We pursue singularity resolution via (1) LQG effective descriptions of the interior, (2) fuzzball construction for non-extremal BHs in string theory, and (3) consistency with the Page curve and island formula. A complete picture would illuminate quantum gravity's deepest regime.

problems.title: Information Loss in Cosmology
problems.tagline: Is information preserved across cosmological horizons?
problems.summary: Extend black hole information results to cosmological settings.
problems.description: De Sitter horizons radiate thermally (Gibbons-Hawking) and pose analogous information questions to black holes. No clear analog of the Page curve exists. Connections to eternal inflation and the multiverse complicate the analysis further.
problems.abstract: This project extends information-preservation frameworks to cosmology via (1) de Sitter replica calculations, (2) identification of observer-dependent entropies in eternal inflation, and (3) operational information-theoretic bounds on cosmological horizons. Progress clarifies cosmological quantum gravity.

problems.title: Nature of Time
problems.tagline: Is time fundamental or emergent?
problems.summary: Determine whether time is a fundamental or derived concept in physics.
problems.description: General relativity treats time as part of spacetime geometry, while quantum mechanics treats it as external parameter. Wheeler-DeWitt equation is timeless. Thermal time hypothesis, Page-Wootters mechanism, and shape dynamics offer various resolutions. None are conclusive.
problems.abstract: We investigate the nature of time through (1) Page-Wootters relational time construction in cosmology, (2) thermal time hypothesis in holographic settings, and (3) operational tests of time's existence in the very early universe. Progress reshapes foundational physics.

problems.title: Measurement Problem in Quantum Mechanics
problems.tagline: How do quantum superpositions become definite outcomes?
problems.summary: Resolve the apparent transition from quantum superposition to classical measurement outcome.
problems.description: Copenhagen, many-worlds, collapse theories, relational, and QBism offer interpretations but make largely indistinguishable predictions. GRW/CSL models predict small deviations testable with ultracold interferometry and macroscopic quantum experiments. A decisive experiment could finally distinguish interpretations.
problems.abstract: This project targets the measurement problem via (1) bounds on spontaneous collapse parameters from LISA Pathfinder, LIGO, and optomechanics, (2) analysis of decoherence timescales in macroscopic systems, and (3) operational tests of observer-independence. Progress could finally discriminate interpretations.

problems.title: Quantum-to-Classical Transition
problems.tagline: How does classical behavior emerge from quantum mechanics?
problems.summary: Characterize the mechanism of classicality emergence from quantum substrates.
problems.description: Decoherence theory explains loss of interference through environmental entanglement, but alone does not explain single outcomes. Quantum Darwinism and einselection address redundancy and stability. Whether additional principles (collapse, consistent histories) are needed remains contested.
problems.abstract: We study the quantum-classical transition via (1) quantum Darwinism experimental tests in photonic systems, (2) bounds on non-linear corrections to Schrödinger dynamics, and (3) theoretical analysis of einselection pointer states. Progress clarifies emergence of classicality.

problems.title: Non-Locality vs Realism
problems.tagline: Which assumption behind Bell inequalities must be abandoned?
problems.summary: Determine whether nature violates locality, realism, or measurement independence.
problems.description: Bell inequality violations rule out local realism, but the specific assumption that fails — locality, realism, or freedom of choice — remains debated. Loophole-free Bell tests confirm violations. Superdeterminism and retrocausal models remain logically viable but philosophically controversial.
problems.abstract: This project analyzes Bell inequality assumptions through (1) improved cosmic-source Bell tests closing freedom-of-choice loophole, (2) analysis of retrocausal and superdeterministic alternatives, and (3) operational tests of local causality. Progress clarifies foundational structure of quantum theory.

problems.title: Complete Theory of Strongly Correlated Electrons
problems.tagline: Find a universal method for strongly correlated electron systems.
problems.summary: Develop a general computational framework for strongly correlated quantum matter.
problems.description: DFT fails for strongly correlated systems (Mott insulators, heavy fermions, high-Tc). Dynamical mean field theory, tensor networks, and quantum embedding provide partial progress. A universal, systematically improvable method remains elusive.
problems.abstract: We pursue strongly-correlated methodology via (1) development of cluster DMFT variants with non-local correlations, (2) tensor network methods scalable to 2D and 3D, and (3) neural-network variational wavefunctions trained on exact small systems. Progress enables predictive correlated materials design.

problems.title: Turbulence Statistical Theory
problems.tagline: Derive intermittency corrections to Kolmogorov scaling.
problems.summary: Establish a complete multifractal theory of 3D turbulent cascades.
problems.description: Kolmogorov 1941 phenomenology predicts E(k) ∝ k⁻⁵/³, with experimentally observed intermittency corrections. Multifractal models (She-Leveque) fit data but lack first-principles derivation. A rigorous theory would complete one of classical physics' oldest puzzles.
problems.abstract: This project targets turbulence statistics via (1) high-Re DNS reaching R_λ > 10³, (2) instanton calculus on Navier-Stokes action for rare events, and (3) renormalization-group analysis of intermittency. Progress resolves a century-old puzzle.

problems.title: Origin of Cosmic Magnetic Fields
problems.tagline: What seeded galactic and intergalactic magnetic fields?
problems.summary: Determine whether cosmic magnetism is primordial or astrophysical in origin.
problems.description: Galaxies exhibit μG magnetic fields; voids show weak intergalactic fields. Primordial generation (inflation, phase transitions) or astrophysical dynamos can explain these. TeV gamma-ray halos around blazars hint at primordial origin. Distinguishing remains challenging.
problems.abstract: We target cosmic magnetogenesis via (1) MHD simulations of galactic dynamos seeded by primordial fields, (2) analysis of blazar halo constraints on intergalactic field strength, and (3) CMB bounds on primordial fields through non-Gaussianity. Resolution informs early-universe physics.

problems.title: Inflationary Initial Conditions
problems.tagline: What set the initial conditions for cosmic inflation?
problems.summary: Determine why and how inflation began.
problems.description: Inflation explains CMB flatness, horizon, and initial perturbations, but requires its own fine-tuned initial conditions (Penrose). Eternal inflation generates multiverse; pre-inflationary physics is largely speculative. The horizon problem is thereby shifted rather than resolved.
problems.abstract: This project addresses inflation's initial conditions through (1) analysis of tunneling from nothing (Hartle-Hawking, Vilenkin), (2) constraints on pre-inflationary universes from CMB tails, and (3) study of eternal inflation measure problems. Progress clarifies cosmic origins.

problems.title: Trans-Planckian Problem
problems.tagline: Do CMB modes require unknown trans-Planckian physics?
problems.summary: Assess sensitivity of inflationary predictions to sub-Planckian physics.
problems.description: CMB perturbation modes observed today corresponded to sub-Planckian wavelengths during inflation. Standard calculations rely on extrapolating QFT into a regime where it should fail. Corrections could appear in CMB non-Gaussianity or spectral index running.
problems.abstract: We address the trans-Planckian problem through (1) analysis of modified dispersion relation impacts on CMB, (2) study of swampland distance conjecture implications, and (3) search for signatures at CMB-S4 and LiteBIRD sensitivities. Progress tests inflation's robustness.

problems.title: Boltzmann Brain Problem
problems.tagline: Why are we not disembodied fluctuations in eternal inflation?
problems.summary: Resolve the cosmological measure problem against Boltzmann brain dominance.
problems.description: In eternal inflation, Boltzmann brains (random thermal fluctuations mimicking observers) eventually dominate statistically. Why are we not one? Proposed resolutions involve measure choices, decaying vacua, or rejection of eternal inflation. None are definitive.
problems.abstract: This project analyzes the Boltzmann brain problem via (1) systematic measure-choice analysis in eternal inflation models, (2) constraints from observed cosmological structure, and (3) falsifiable implications for dark energy equation of state. Progress clarifies self-locating probabilities in cosmology.

problems.title: Complete AdS/CFT Dictionary
problems.tagline: Fully decode the bulk from boundary CFT data.
problems.summary: Complete the holographic dictionary between AdS gravity and boundary CFT.
problems.description: AdS/CFT maps boundary CFT operators to bulk fields, but sub-AdS locality, interior reconstruction, and precise encoding of bulk details remain partial. Modular flow, quantum error correction, and complexity conjectures offer new tools but completion requires more work.
problems.abstract: We extend the holographic dictionary via (1) explicit interior reconstruction via modular flow in JT+matter, (2) refinement of holographic complexity proposals, and (3) sub-AdS locality from entanglement wedge methods. Progress completes holography's foundation.

problems.title: Swampland Distance Conjecture Proof
problems.tagline: Prove distances in moduli space produce infinite light towers.
problems.summary: Establish the swampland distance conjecture from first principles.
problems.description: The conjecture asserts that infinite distance limits in moduli space produce infinite towers of light states. Empirically supported across all known string vacua. A first-principles proof would constrain all consistent quantum gravities.
problems.abstract: This project targets the swampland distance conjecture through (1) systematic checks in non-geometric string backgrounds, (2) connection to topological recursion on moduli spaces, and (3) implications for inflation and dark energy. A proof would substantially advance the swampland program.

problems.title: M-Theory Formulation
problems.tagline: What are the fundamental degrees of freedom of M-theory?
problems.summary: Formulate M-theory's underlying dynamical degrees of freedom.
problems.description: M-theory unifies five string theories and 11D supergravity via dualities, but its fundamental degrees of freedom are unknown. BFSS and IKKT matrix models provide partial glimpses. Membrane and M5-brane dynamics resist direct quantization.
problems.abstract: This project pursues M-theory formulation via (1) further study of BFSS matrix model as M-theory on light cone, (2) quantization of M2-brane on curved backgrounds, and (3) holographic duals of M-theory black holes. A complete formulation would close a foundational gap.

problems.title: Realistic String Vacuum
problems.tagline: Find the Standard Model in the string landscape.
problems.summary: Construct an explicit string compactification reproducing the full Standard Model.
problems.description: String theory vacua with Standard Model gauge group and matter content exist but reproducing exact couplings, masses, and mixings remains elusive. Heterotic, F-theory, and type IIB flux vacua each offer partial successes. A fully realistic vacuum would validate string phenomenology.
problems.abstract: We target realistic string vacua via (1) machine-learning-assisted search over heterotic and F-theory configurations, (2) precision calculation of Yukawa couplings and Kahler potentials, and (3) statistical analysis of the Standard Model's position in the landscape. Success would realize string phenomenology's central goal.

problems.title: Rigorous QFT in 4D
problems.tagline: Construct an interacting 4D quantum field theory rigorously.
problems.summary: Establish mathematical existence of a non-trivial interacting quantum field theory in 4 dimensions.
problems.description: Constructive QFT has succeeded in 2D and 3D for φ⁴ and Yukawa models. In 4D, φ⁴ is widely believed trivial (no interacting continuum limit). Yang-Mills existence is a Millennium Prize problem. A rigorous interacting 4D QFT remains one of mathematical physics' central open problems.
problems.abstract: This project targets 4D constructive QFT via (1) rigorous proof of φ⁴₄ triviality, (2) extension of Balaban's lattice approach to Yang-Mills continuum limit, and (3) stochastic quantization methods on the full lattice. Progress addresses the Yang-Mills Millennium problem.

problems.title: S-Matrix Bootstrap Completion
problems.tagline: Determine QFTs from their S-matrix consistency alone.
problems.summary: Complete the S-matrix bootstrap program for determining quantum field theories.
problems.description: The modern S-matrix bootstrap uses unitarity, analyticity, and crossing symmetry to constrain scattering amplitudes. It has yielded non-trivial bounds on Wilson coefficients and allowed parameter-free 2D QCD solutions. Extending to 4D amplitudes is an active frontier.
problems.abstract: We advance the S-matrix bootstrap through (1) semidefinite programming bounds on 4D graviton amplitudes, (2) analytic bounds on EFT Wilson coefficients, and (3) integration with CFT bootstrap methods. Progress may yield new non-perturbative handles on gravity and strong interactions.

problems.title: Derivation of Statistical Mechanics from Quantum Mechanics
problems.tagline: Derive equilibrium statistical mechanics from isolated quantum dynamics.
problems.summary: Provide a rigorous derivation of thermal equilibrium from quantum mechanics.
problems.description: Typicality arguments (von Neumann, Goldstein et al.) and ETH suggest equilibrium emerges for most initial states. Rigorous proofs for interacting systems are limited. The foundations of thermodynamics from first-principles QM remain incomplete.
problems.abstract: This project targets rigorous derivation of statistical mechanics via (1) typicality theorems for random Hamiltonians, (2) ETH proofs in disordered free-fermion limits, and (3) quantum thermalization rates from Lieb-Robinson bounds. Progress clarifies thermodynamic foundations.

problems.title: Complete Theory of Quantum Phase Transitions
problems.tagline: Build a unified framework for continuous quantum phase transitions.
problems.summary: Develop a universal theory for quantum critical points in strongly correlated systems.
problems.description: Quantum critical points govern low-temperature phase diagrams of correlated materials. Sachdev-Ye-Kitaev models have added non-Fermi-liquid insights. A universal framework analogous to classical Landau-Ginzburg remains incomplete, particularly at metallic QCPs.
problems.abstract: We pursue quantum critical theory via (1) Eliashberg-like treatments of metallic QCPs with SYK insights, (2) numerical tests on frustrated Kondo lattices, and (3) classification of universality classes beyond Landau paradigm. Progress clarifies quantum criticality in real materials.

problems.title: Origin of Arrow of Time
problems.tagline: Why does entropy increase in the direction of time?
problems.summary: Explain the thermodynamic arrow of time from microphysical laws.
problems.description: Microphysical laws are time-reversal symmetric, yet thermodynamic entropy increases. The past hypothesis invokes a low-entropy Big Bang, shifting the question to early-universe cosmology. Explanations range from inflation to quantum cosmology initial conditions.
problems.abstract: This project examines the arrow of time through (1) analysis of initial conditions in eternal inflation scenarios, (2) decoherence and branching-structure analysis in many-worlds, and (3) operational tests of time-reversal in mesoscopic systems. Progress clarifies a deep foundational puzzle.

problems.title: Hawking Radiation Beyond Semiclassical
problems.tagline: Compute backreaction and correlations in Hawking radiation.
problems.summary: Extend Hawking radiation computation beyond leading-order semiclassical approximation.
problems.description: Hawking's original calculation is semiclassical. Backreaction effects and information-encoding correlations require going beyond. Island formula provides partial answers; complete calculation demands full quantum gravity.
problems.abstract: We extend Hawking radiation computations via (1) backreaction-corrected spectra in JT gravity and its CGHS generalizations, (2) explicit correlation functions encoding information across the Page curve, and (3) lessons for higher-dimensional BH evaporation. Progress sharpens our understanding of BH information transfer.

problems.title: Casimir Effect in Curved Spacetime
problems.tagline: Compute Casimir energies in generic curved backgrounds.
problems.summary: Establish regularization and renormalization of Casimir energies on arbitrary curved manifolds.
problems.description: Casimir calculations in flat space are well-understood. In curved spacetimes, regularization ambiguities and non-trivial boundary contributions complicate matters. Applications range from cosmology (dark energy contributions) to braneworld scenarios.
problems.abstract: This project addresses Casimir effects on curved backgrounds via (1) zeta-function regularization on cosmological metrics, (2) bound on contributions to effective cosmological constant, and (3) implications for braneworld stabilization. Progress informs vacuum-energy contributions.

problems.title: Complete Understanding of Dualities
problems.tagline: What underlying principle unifies S-, T-, and mirror dualities?
problems.summary: Derive string dualities from a fundamental organizing principle.
problems.description: S-duality, T-duality, and mirror symmetry relate seemingly distinct quantum theories. Their universality suggests a deeper structure. Categorical, topological, and arithmetic frameworks are emerging but incomplete.
problems.abstract: We pursue unified understanding of dualities via (1) categorical formulation of mirror symmetry extended to higher dimensions, (2) derived-algebraic-geometry analysis of Langlands correspondences from dualities, and (3) physical applications to non-perturbative QCD. Progress reveals string theory's hidden organizing principles.

problems.title: Non-Perturbative Effects in Gauge Theories
problems.tagline: Classify all non-perturbative configurations in gauge theories.
problems.summary: Provide a complete classification of instantons, monopoles, and domain walls in gauge theories.
problems.description: Instantons, monopoles, and domain walls drive non-perturbative gauge dynamics. Resurgence theory connects perturbative and non-perturbative sectors. Complete classification in 4D interacting theories remains out of reach.
problems.abstract: This project advances non-perturbative gauge theory via (1) resurgence analysis of N=2* theories, (2) instanton sums in theories with center symmetry, and (3) lattice verification of semi-classical predictions. Progress clarifies the non-perturbative structure of gauge theories.

problems.title: Fate of the False Vacuum
problems.tagline: Is our vacuum metastable, and what determines its decay rate?
problems.summary: Compute vacuum decay rates for the Standard Model Higgs potential.
problems.description: The Standard Model Higgs potential, extrapolated to high scales, suggests metastability. Coleman-De Luccia decay would nucleate bubbles of true vacuum expanding at light speed. Quantum gravity corrections and new physics at intermediate scales can stabilize or destabilize.
problems.abstract: We investigate vacuum decay via (1) precision Higgs potential calculations including gravitational corrections, (2) analysis of new-physics contributions near the instability scale, and (3) cosmological implications of bubble nucleation histories. Progress informs the ultimate fate of our universe.

problems.title: Arithmetic Structure of Fundamental Constants
problems.tagline: Derive fundamental constants from an arithmetic Golden Chain.
problems.summary: Establish the ASRP Golden Chain framework linking φ, ℚ(√5), ζ-zeros, and physical couplings.
problems.description: The ASRP program proposes a Golden Chain: golden ratio φ → quadratic field ℚ(√5) → Riemann zeta zeros → physical couplings (α, quark/lepton masses). This arithmetic structure recasts fundamental constants as consequences of number theory. A complete derivation would resolve why constants take their observed values.
problems.abstract: This project formalizes and tests the ASRP Golden Chain. We will (1) rigorously define the arithmetic flow from φ through ℚ(√5) to the BC spectrum, (2) derive quark/lepton mass ratios from Golden Chain first principles, and (3) compute higher-order corrections reconciling 0.04% deviations in α⁻¹ = 360/φ². Success would provide a number-theoretic origin for all Standard Model parameters.

problems.title: Theory of Everything
problems.tagline: Unify all forces and matter into a single framework.
problems.summary: Construct a single consistent theory describing all fundamental phenomena.
problems.description: String theory, loop quantum gravity, and asymptotic safety are among candidates. None achieves the combined goals of mathematical consistency, unique prediction, and empirical confirmation. Whether humanity can formulate such a theory is itself unclear.
problems.abstract: This survey project maps the landscape of TOE candidates against criteria of (1) mathematical completeness, (2) predictive uniqueness, and (3) empirical adequacy. We will develop falsifiability criteria for each and propose experimental routes to discriminate. Progress reshapes fundamental physics.

problems.title: Why Does Anything Exist?
problems.tagline: Why is there something rather than nothing?
problems.summary: Address the question of why a universe exists at all.
problems.description: Hartle-Hawking no-boundary proposal and Vilenkin tunneling-from-nothing offer quantum-cosmological frameworks for spontaneous universe creation. Whether 'nothing' in these contexts is truly nothing remains philosophically fraught. This is physics' most extreme foundational question.
problems.abstract: We examine proposed quantum origins of the universe via (1) careful analysis of Hartle-Hawking wave function with realistic matter content, (2) comparison with Vilenkin's tunneling approach, and (3) consistency checks against observed CMB features. Progress touches philosophy of physics' deepest question.

problems.title: Why These Particular Laws of Physics?
problems.tagline: Why does nature obey these specific laws?
problems.summary: Explain why physical laws take their observed form.
problems.description: Anthropic reasoning suggests that only certain laws permit observers, biasing selection. Alternative: some deeper principle dictates uniqueness. The string landscape populates many possibilities, and we observe one compatible with our existence.
problems.abstract: This project contrasts anthropic and dynamical selection principles by (1) surveying observable consequences of landscape population, (2) analyzing swampland constraints on self-consistent laws, and (3) critique of anthropic reasoning's falsifiability. Progress reframes fundamental-law selection.

problems.title: Why 3+1 Dimensions?
problems.tagline: Why do we observe three spatial and one time dimension?
problems.summary: Explain the dimensionality of observed spacetime.
problems.description: String theory requires 10 or 11 dimensions, with extras compactified. Anthropic arguments (stable orbits, wave propagation) constrain dimensionality. Brandenberger-Vafa string gas cosmology offers dynamical selection of 3+1. No definitive answer exists.
