problems.abstract: We examine dimensionality selection via (1) detailed analysis of Brandenberger-Vafa string gas, (2) anthropic constraints on chemistry and astrophysics in alternative dimensions, and (3) swampland implications for compactification. Progress clarifies spacetime dimensionality.

problems.title: Nature of Consciousness and Physics
problems.tagline: Does physics underlie consciousness?
problems.summary: Determine whether consciousness admits a physical explanation.
problems.description: Integrated Information Theory, Penrose-Hameroff orchestrated objective reduction, and Global Workspace Theory attempt physical explanations of consciousness. None is falsifiable at current experimental precision. The hard problem of consciousness likely transcends current physics.
problems.abstract: This project surveys physics-based consciousness theories against (1) falsifiability criteria, (2) empirical testability via EEG/MEG correlates, and (3) compatibility with neuroscience. A scientific resolution may require new frameworks beyond current physics.

problems.title: Predictability of Quantum Gravity
problems.tagline: Does quantum gravity make unique predictions?
problems.summary: Assess whether quantum gravity will be predictive or ambiguous.
problems.description: String landscape hosts ~10⁵⁰⁰ vacua, challenging predictivity. Alternative approaches (LQG, asymptotic safety) may be more constrained but lack empirical support. Whether quantum gravity can make unique empirical predictions is uncertain.
problems.abstract: We analyze quantum-gravity predictivity by (1) statistical analysis of Standard Model's position in landscape, (2) comparison of predictions across LQG, strings, asymptotic safety, and (3) possible observational discriminators (primordial GWs, BH quasi-normal modes). Progress frames quantum gravity's empirical status.

problems.title: Resolution of Cosmological Measure Problem
problems.tagline: How to compute probabilities in an infinite multiverse.
problems.summary: Define meaningful probabilities in eternally inflating multiverse scenarios.
problems.description: In eternal inflation, all outcomes occur infinitely often, making probabilities ill-defined. Proposed measures (causal patch, scale factor cutoff, comoving) give different answers and often ad hoc. A principled resolution would enable predictions in multiverse scenarios.
problems.abstract: This project surveys and compares cosmological measures against (1) consistency with observed cosmological parameters, (2) freedom from paradoxes (Boltzmann brain, youngness problem), and (3) theoretical motivations. Progress may enable multiverse phenomenology.

problems.title: Pre-Big-Bang Physics
problems.tagline: What preceded the Big Bang?
problems.summary: Characterize the cosmological epoch preceding inflation.
problems.description: Bouncing cosmologies, cyclic universes, and pre-big-bang string scenarios propose pre-Big-Bang epochs. Observational discrimination is hard. Each faces theoretical challenges (BKL oscillations, ekpyrotic entropy).
problems.abstract: We examine pre-Big-Bang models via (1) detailed analysis of CMB signatures of bouncing scenarios, (2) consistency checks against BKL singularity behavior, and (3) implications for arrow of time. Progress informs earliest cosmic history.

problems.title: Ultimate Fate of the Universe
problems.tagline: Heat death, big rip, or vacuum decay?
problems.summary: Predict the long-term cosmological fate of the universe.
problems.description: The universe's fate depends on dark energy equation of state and vacuum stability. Heat death follows from ΛCDM; Big Rip from phantom dark energy; vacuum decay from metastability. Future surveys will tighten w(z) constraints.
problems.abstract: This project contrasts fate scenarios via (1) projected DESI + Euclid constraints on w(z), (2) Higgs vacuum stability analysis at future colliders, and (3) implications for civilizational longevity. Progress addresses physics' ultimate question.

problems.title: Is Physics Ultimately Computable?
problems.tagline: Does the Church-Turing thesis apply to physics?
problems.summary: Determine whether physical processes can be simulated by Turing machines.
problems.description: Quantum field theories and gravitation may harbor uncomputable phenomena. Spectral gap undecidability (Cubitt-Perez-Garcia-Wolf) and related results suggest physics contains genuinely uncomputable features. Implications for simulation and predictability are profound.
problems.abstract: We analyze computability of physics via (1) extension of spectral gap undecidability to physically realistic systems, (2) analysis of Wang tilings and quantum cellular automata complexity, and (3) implications for physics simulation. Progress illuminates physics' algorithmic limits.

problems.title: Nature of Spacetime Singularities
problems.tagline: What is the structure of singularities in quantum gravity?
problems.summary: Characterize the quantum-gravitational structure of classical spacetime singularities.
problems.description: BKL conjecture describes classical singularity approach with chaotic mixmaster behavior. Quantum resolution may yield bounces, fuzzballs, or baby universes. No complete framework exists.
problems.abstract: This project studies singularity resolution through (1) mini-superspace quantization of BKL dynamics, (2) holographic reconstruction of fuzzball interiors, and (3) LQG effective descriptions. Progress informs quantum gravity's hardest regime.

problems.title: Complete Theory of Quantum Cosmology
problems.tagline: Construct a quantum theory of the universe as a whole.
problems.summary: Develop a predictive quantum mechanics applicable to the entire universe.
problems.description: Wheeler-DeWitt equation treats the universe's wave function formally, but lacks clear interpretation (no external observer). Decoherent histories and consistent histories attempt resolutions. Full operational predictivity remains elusive.
problems.abstract: We pursue quantum cosmology via (1) analysis of Wheeler-DeWitt solutions in realistic models, (2) consistent histories approach to cosmological probabilities, and (3) implications for observed CMB features. Progress clarifies quantum cosmology's foundations.

problems.title: Holographic Principle for Cosmological Spacetimes
problems.tagline: Extend holography from AdS to de Sitter cosmology.
problems.summary: Establish a holographic description of our accelerating universe.
problems.description: AdS/CFT provides holographic duality for negative cosmological constant. For de Sitter (our universe), no analogous dual exists. dS/CFT proposals are tentative. Entropy bounds and observer-dependent horizons complicate the picture.
problems.abstract: This project pursues cosmological holography via (1) sharp formulation of dS/CFT with matter content, (2) holographic interpretation of CMB observables, and (3) information-theoretic bounds on observer's past light cone. Progress would extend holography to our universe.

problems.title: Is the Multiverse Real?
problems.tagline: Is the multiverse scientific or metaphysical?
problems.summary: Determine whether the multiverse hypothesis is empirically meaningful.
problems.description: Eternal inflation and string landscape predict a multiverse, but other universes are causally disconnected. Proposed observational signatures (bubble collisions, anthropic predictions) are extraordinarily subtle. Whether this crosses the scientific-metaphysical boundary is debated.
problems.abstract: We assess multiverse scientific status by (1) searching for bubble collision signatures in CMB, (2) analyzing anthropic predictions' testability, and (3) philosophical analysis of falsifiability criteria. Progress frames multiverse's empirical content.

problems.title: Can Quantum Mechanics Be Derived from Deeper Principles?
problems.tagline: Derive quantum mechanics from information-theoretic axioms.
problems.summary: Reconstruct quantum mechanics from more fundamental principles.
problems.description: Hardy, Chiribella-D'Ariano-Perinotti, and Masanes-Müller programs derive QM from operational axioms (continuous reversibility, local tomography, purification). None is universally accepted. A canonical derivation would clarify QM's status.
problems.abstract: This project examines QM reconstructions by (1) comparing axiom sets across Hardy, CDP, and Masanes-Müller, (2) identifying the minimal required assumptions, and (3) exploring alternative information-theoretic frameworks. Progress would illuminate QM's essential character.

problems.title: Emergence of Classicality from Quantum Substrate
problems.tagline: How does classical reality arise from quantum mechanics?
problems.summary: Establish a complete account of classical emergence from quantum substrate.
problems.description: Decoherence, einselection, and quantum Darwinism explain many aspects of classicality. Yet a fully satisfactory account explaining single outcomes and definite experiences remains contested. Related to the measurement problem.
problems.abstract: We pursue classicality emergence through (1) quantum Darwinism experimental tests, (2) analysis of branching structures in many-worlds, and (3) operational criteria for classical regime onset. Progress clarifies quantum-to-classical transition.

problems.title: Complete Theory of Quantum Reference Frames
problems.tagline: Treat reference frames as quantum systems themselves.
problems.summary: Develop a complete quantum theory of reference frames.
problems.description: Physical reference frames are ultimately quantum. Page-Wootters mechanism treats time via internal clock; recent extensions cover position and rotation. A complete relational framework for all symmetries is incomplete.
problems.abstract: This project develops quantum reference frame theory via (1) extension of Page-Wootters to full Lorentz symmetry, (2) implications for gauge-invariant observables, and (3) operational tests in quantum optics. Progress clarifies relational quantum foundations.

problems.title: Physics of the Planck Scale
problems.tagline: What does physics look like at 10⁻³⁵ m?
problems.summary: Characterize physical processes at the Planck scale.
problems.description: At the Planck scale, quantum fluctuations of spacetime dominate. Standard QFT and GR both fail. Direct experimental access is impossible; only theoretical modeling and indirect signals (trans-Planckian CMB, gravitational waves) are available.
problems.abstract: We approach Planck-scale physics via (1) effective field theory of quantum gravity at sub-Planck scales, (2) signatures of Planck-scale phenomena in observable CMB and GW data, and (3) theoretical models from string theory and LQG. Progress constrains quantum gravity empirically.

problems.title: Mathematical Consistency of Quantum Field Theory
problems.tagline: Is QFT fundamentally mathematically consistent?
problems.summary: Establish or refute full mathematical consistency of relativistic QFT.
problems.description: Haag's theorem suggests interaction picture QFT is mathematically ill-defined. Renormalization recovers physics but at the cost of rigor. Constructive QFT has succeeded only in low dimensions. Whether 4D interacting QFT is consistent remains open.
problems.abstract: This project addresses QFT consistency by (1) analysis of Haag's theorem implications for physical QFT, (2) constructive QFT program updates in 4D, and (3) rigorous renormalization group flows. Progress is central to mathematical physics.

problems.title: Why Is the Universe Comprehensible?
problems.tagline: Wigner's unreasonable effectiveness of mathematics.
problems.summary: Explain why mathematics is so effective in describing the physical world.
problems.description: Wigner's famous essay posed why mathematics, an abstract human construction, so effectively describes physics. Possible answers include cognitive evolution, mathematical realism (Tegmark's mathematical universe), or simple selection bias. None is definitive.
problems.abstract: This project examines the comprehensibility question via (1) historical analysis of mathematical-physical correspondences, (2) Tegmark's mathematical universe hypothesis and its testability, and (3) cognitive science perspectives on mathematical intuition. Progress may remain philosophical.
